summary: The test suite failed one test file (test_tcl) due to two assertion failures where Tcl string results were mojibaked (decoded with the wrong encoding) instead of producing the expected Unicode characters.
root_cause: test.test_tcl.TclTest tests failed because Tcl-returned characters were decoded incorrectly (mojibake) â€” the test expected a Unicode code point but received CP1252-decoded bytes instead.

evidence.reason: Shows the specific test that failed.
Shows the second specific failing test in the same test file.
Concrete assertion failure: expected a surrogate/Unicode escape form but got mojibaked characters ('Ã°Å¸â€™Â»'), indicating an encoding/decoding mismatch.
The Python process locale encoding is cp1252, which can cause UTF-8 bytes to appear as mojibake when decoded with the wrong codec.
Shows the Tcl version in use during the tests (relevant because the failure arises from communication with the Tcl library).
The build used an external Tcl/Tk distribution (tcltk-9.0.3.0), confirming the test exercised the embedded Tcl implementation.

suggested_fix: Ensure Tcl results are decoded as UTF-8 (not the system code page) when converting Tcl strings to Python strings. Concretely: run the tests with a UTF-8 locale (so locale.getencoding returns 'utf-8') or modify the _tkinter/Tcl result decoding to explicitly use 'utf-8' for Tcl-returned byte sequences. Re-run the failing test file (Lib\test\test_tcl.py) after applying the change.
